summary: Dependency-confusion beacon to hardcoded bare-IP endpoint on postinstall
details: package.json declares a postinstall hook (postinstall.js) that performs an HTTP GET to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint over plain HTTP (http://130.49.177.51:18080/p/dc-20260627-yandex-geobase) carrying the package name, version, and a fixed nonce when the package is installed. The package self-identifies in its description, README, and an exported __dependency_confusion_poc__ flag as a dependency-confusion test targeting an internal/private name ('yandex-geobase'). Any installer (typically an internal build) that resolves this public name on `npm install` will automatically execute the lifecycle script and report installation to the third-party endpoint, confirming the existence of the internal package name and the host's reachability to the attacker-chosen IP. Regardless of the 'security test' framing, publishing a name-shadowing package to the public registry that beacons install events from arbitrary downstream installers constitutes unauthorized install-time exfiltration against those installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
